output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 6/6.
Batch 1: Implemented batch T1 in the resize math layer. `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts` now exports `ClipEdgeResizeContext`, `applyClipEdgeMove`, and `snapBoundaryToSiblings`, with the dispatcher reusing `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize` for interior and outer paths and normalizing free/interior/outer results into clip update arrays. `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts` now covers free, interior, and outer dispatcher behavior plus clamp and snap cases. Verified by running the full `resize-math` test module successfully.
Batch 2: Implemented batch T2 in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`. The canvas now uses one `ClipEdgeResizeSession`, resolves resize context plus sibling snap times up front via `resolveClipEdgeResizeContext(...)`, and runs both `updateResize` and `endResize` through the same dispatcher-backed preview path built on `applyClipEdgeMove`. Preview overrides for grouped outer edges are derived from returned child updates, the temporary `[resize-diag]` log is removed, and the shared end path now falls back to the live preview snapshot when `lostpointercapture` arrives without a usable `clientX`. Verified with the full `TimelineCanvas` test module and structural grep checks for the removed legacy resize-kind strings.
Batch 3: Implemented batch T3 by renaming the resize commit surface to `onClipEdgeResizeEnd({ session, updates, cancelled })` across the hook, timeline state types/contexts, editor wiring, provider wiring, and canvas handoff. `useClipResize` now has one shared commit path driven by the unified session plus dispatcher updates, and the legacy `startGroupEdgeResize`, `getInteriorNeighbor`, `resolveGroupMembers`, `onActionResizeEnd`, and `onShotGroupEdgeResize` wiring is removed from the named implementation files. Structural grep passed, the filtered `tsc` scan produced no errors in the changed T3 files, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/collapse-the-three-parallel-20260408-1934/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Implemented batch T4 by rewriting the resize hook and canvas tests to the unified `onClipEdgeResizeEnd({ session, updates, cancelled })` contract. The hook tests now cover free, interior, and outer commit paths, the existing grouped/off-center canvas assertions were preserved against the new callback payload, and a new end-to-end free-clip resize test verifies sibling snapping on release. Both full test modules pass, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/collapse-the-three-parallel-20260408-1934/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Completed batch T5 validation. The throwaway repro script was created, run successfully against the off-center free-edge, off-center interior-boundary, and free-clip snap-to-sibling scenarios, then deleted. `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, the combined `useClipResize.test.tsx` + `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` run, and the broader `src/tools/video-editor` suite all passed. Structural greps for `kind: 'group-child-boundary'`, `kind: 'group-edge'`, `startGroupEdgeResize`, and `[resize-diag]` in the resize lifecycle/wiring files returned zero hits. The narrowed `TimelineCanvas` lifecycle block count (`updateResize`, `endResize`, `handleResizePointerDown`) decreased from `418` lines at `HEAD` to `389` lines currently. Full app `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` still fails due unrelated pre-existing errors outside `src/tools/video-editor/`, while a filtered scan for `src/tools/video-editor/` returned no matches. The checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/collapse-the-three-parallel-20260408-1934/execution_batch_5.json`.
Batch 6: Completed batch T6 by rerunning the full `src/tools/video-editor` vitest suite on the final working tree. The verification pass succeeded with `42/42` test files and `227/227` tests passing, and no additional regressions were found or fixed in this batch. Checkpointed the result to `.megaplan/plans/collapse-the-three-parallel-20260408-1934/execution_batch_6.json`.
commands_run: if [ -e .megaplan/plans/collapse-the-three-parallel-20260408-1934/execution_batch_6.json ]; then echo exists; else echo missing; fi
deviations: Used `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts ...` because bare `vitest` was not on PATH in this workspace.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `ClipEdgeResizeContext`, `applyClipEdgeMove`, and `snapBoundaryToSiblings` in `resize-math.ts`, keeping grouped resize behavior delegated to `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize`. Expanded `resize-math.test.ts` to cover free, interior, and outer contexts plus clamp and snap behavior, then verified with the full `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts` module run.

Collapsed `TimelineCanvas.tsx` to a single `ClipEdgeResizeSession` plus `resolveClipEdgeResizeContext` and a shared `computeResizePreview` path. `updateResize` and `endResize` now share dispatcher-based preview math, sibling snap inputs are fixed at pointer-down, grouped outer preview overrides are derived from returned child updates, the temporary `[resize-diag]` log is removed, and lost-pointer-capture finalization falls back to the live preview snapshot when `clientX` is unavailable. Verified with the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module and structural grep checks for the removed legacy resize-kind strings.

Renamed the resize callback surface to `onClipEdgeResizeEnd({ session, updates, cancelled })` through the hook, timeline state types/contexts, editor wiring, provider wiring, and canvas handoff. Removed `startGroupEdgeResize`, `getInteriorNeighbor`, and `resolveGroupMembers`, and collapsed `useClipResize` to one dispatcher-fed commit path that branches only on `session.context.kind` for metadata/origin handling. Structural grep confirms the legacy callback and helper names are gone from the named wiring files. Verification: `useClipResize.test.tsx` and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` currently fail because they still target the deleted pre-rename API surface; those test rewrites are the explicit scope of T4. A filtered `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` scan produced no errors in the changed T3 files.

Rewrote `useClipResize.test.tsx` around the unified `onClipEdgeResizeEnd({ session, updates, cancelled })` contract and updated `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` to assert the new callback payload without weakening the existing grouped and off-center resize expectations. Added a new end-to-end free-clip resize integration case that verifies sibling snapping on release. Verified by running the full `useClipResize.test.tsx` and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` modules successfully.

Ran a short throwaway repro script for the off-center free-edge, off-center interior-boundary, and free-clip snap-to-sibling cases, confirmed the expected boundary-tracking outputs, and deleted the script afterward. Verified `resize-math.test.ts`, the combined `useClipResize.test.tsx` + `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` run, and the broader `src/tools/video-editor` vitest suite successfully (`42` files / `227` tests passed). Structural grep over the resize lifecycle and wiring files returned zero hits for `kind: 'group-child-boundary'`, `kind: 'group-edge'`, `startGroupEdgeResize`, and `[resize-diag]`. A targeted line-count comparison over the `TimelineCanvas` resize lifecycle blocks (`updateResize`, `endResize`, `handleResizePointerDown`) shows a net drop from `418` lines at `HEAD` to `389` lines currently. Full `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` still fails due unrelated pre-existing errors outside the resize slice, but a filtered scan for `src/tools/video-editor/` returned no matches.
commands_run: node - <<'NODE' ... TimelineCanvas lifecycle block line-count comparison ... NODE

task_updates.executor_notes: Reran the full `src/tools/video-editor` vitest suite against the final working tree to verify the changed resize files and surrounding editor surface together. The suite passed (`42` files / `227` tests), and no additional regressions were found after the T5 validation fixes.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `resize-math.ts` now exports `ClipEdgeResizeContext`, `applyClipEdgeMove`, and `snapBoundaryToSiblings`. Unit coverage exercises free, interior, and outer dispatch paths plus clamp and snap behavior, while reusing the existing grouped resize math helpers instead of duplicating them.

`TimelineCanvas.tsx` now uses a single `ClipEdgeResizeSession`, `resolveClipEdgeResizeContext(...)` precomputes `context` plus `siblingTimes` at pointer-down, and both `updateResize` and `endResize` run through the same dispatcher-backed compute path. Structural grep confirms the legacy resize-kind strings and the temporary `[resize-diag]` log are gone from the lifecycle code.

The callback surface is renamed to `onClipEdgeResizeEnd({ session, updates, cancelled })` across the named wiring files, `startGroupEdgeResize`, `getInteriorNeighbor`, and `resolveGroupMembers` are removed from implementation code, and `useClipResize` now commits from the unified dispatcher output rather than rediscovering resize geometry. The remaining failures are downstream tests that still reference the deleted pre-rename API and are scheduled for rewrite in T4.

The hook tests now cover the unified free, interior, and outer commit paths via `onClipEdgeResizeEnd`, the canvas integration tests preserve the existing grouped/off-center behavior assertions using the new callback payload, and a new end-to-end free-clip case verifies snap-to-sibling behavior on release.

The throwaway repro script was created, run successfully against the off-center and snap scenarios, and deleted. The required test commands passed for `resize-math`, the rewritten hook/canvas modules, and the full `src/tools/video-editor` suite, structural greps were clean, and the narrowed `TimelineCanvas` lifecycle block count decreased from `418` lines at `HEAD` to `389` lines currently. Full app `tsc` still reports unrelated pre-existing failures outside `src/tools/video-editor/`, while the filtered scan for `src/tools/video-editor/` returned no matches.

The verification tests passed on a fresh rerun of the full `src/tools/video-editor` suite (`42` files / `227` tests). No additional regressions were found and no further fixes were required after the T5 validation pass.
